sae_features.label: Clan CD cysteine protease domains
sae_features.description: Cysteine endopeptidase catalytic domains of the caspase/legumain-like (clan CD) fold—covering caspases, metacaspases, paracaspases, and legumain—capturing the conserved core and zymogen-processing architecture rather than the specific catalytic residues.

sae_features.label: Domain-boundary entry activation segments
sae_features.description: Boundary/activation segment at the start of structured domains—most prominently the junction from propeptide/linker into caspase-family cysteine peptidase cores (p20) but generalizing to equivalent N-terminal entry patches of diverse folds, both catalytic (e.g., PDEase, Toprim, amidase) and signaling (e.g., SEFIR)—highlighting regulatory linkers and the first helices/strands while largely avoiding catalytic/ligand-binding residues.

sae_features.label: C-terminal active-site buttress
sae_features.description: Active-site–proximal C-terminal subdomain of hydrolase catalytic domains, most strongly the small/p10-like region of caspase-like (clan CD) cysteine proteases; the feature targets the helix/loops that buttress the catalytic His/Cys/Ser and often include autoinhibitory or processing elements.

sae_features.label: Catalytic His-Gly loop
sae_features.description: A short, glycine/acidic–enriched catalytic loop (often carrying a contiguous His–Gly, HG) that sits immediately adjacent to active sites or metal‑binding histidines, marking the functional loop that flanks/bridges catalytic residues across diverse enzymes (especially peptidases/hydrolases) and, by extension, analogous glycine‑rich signaling loops in SEFIR/TIR‑like domains.

sae_features.label: Caspase-like C-terminal lobe
sae_features.description: Caspase-like cysteine protease fold (caspase-hemoglobinase, Clan CD), with strongest signal for the C-terminal small subunit/p10 (or C-terminal lobe) and its neighboring active-site/dimer-interface loops; recognizes both catalytic and pseudo-catalytic members (caspases, separases, metacaspases, paracaspases, and the related GPI-anchor transamidase PIG-K), and also shows secondary strong activation on certain papain-like cysteine proteases (Clan CA; e.g., gingipains); often extends into adjacent flexible, basic/glycine-rich C-terminal tails; occasional cross-activation on unrelated C-terminal low-complexity segments.

sae_features.label: Caspase-like cysteine protease core
sae_features.description: A domain-level signal for caspase-like cysteine endopeptidase catalytic cores and their zymogen-processing architecture: it centers on Cys–His catalytic dyads within the p20/p10-type subunit region of caspases/metacaspases/paracaspases and the homologous catalytic module of separase, and generalizes to related exported protease domains; rare secondary matches occur in non-protease enzymes that present a similar local strand–loop–helix microenvironment.

sae_features.label: Beta-loop-alpha active-site junctions
sae_features.description: Functional beta-strand–loop–alpha-helix junction segments that form “active/interaction loops” within catalytic or signaling domains, often adjacent to active sites or binding interfaces; commonly seen in hydrolases (including proteases, amidases, glycosidases, deacetylases) and TIR/SEFIR-like signaling modules, with frequent acidic/glycine residues and occasional proximity to disulfides or zymogen regions

sae_features.label: Caspase-like cysteine protease core
sae_features.description: Cysteine protease catalytic core and zymogen‑processing segment of caspase‑like enzymes: the feature recognizes the His–Cys dyad environment and the adjacent activation/cleavage loop (e.g., the p20/p10 linker in caspases), generalizing to related cysteine transamidases/proteases with homologous active‑site architecture.
